Metadata.title: millisecond — Historical Formula Racing Simulator
Metadata.description: Build a legendary team, stay under the Budget Cap and simulate a championship decided by milliseconds.

strength.carTrackFit: The aerodynamic package was decisive, especially through the corners at {circuitName}.
strength.engineTrackFit: The raw engine power on the straights made the difference.
strength.driverPairSynergy: The great synergy between the drivers maximized the result.
strength.reliability: The robustness of the equipment endured the race.

weakness.carTrackFit: However, the car struggled with extreme lack of grip.
weakness.engineTrackFit: Nonetheless, the engine fell behind rivals on the straights.
weakness.driverPairSynergy: Internal tension between the duo compromised the race.
weakness.reliability: But reliability was a critical weak point.
weakness.errorRisk: And a high risk of driver errors compromised the event.

championship.brilliant: A brilliant season! Team {teamName} delivered consistent results, maximizing the equipment.
championship.difficult: A very difficult season for {teamName}, struggling to score points despite the investment.
championship.average: Team {teamName}'s season was average, reflecting a balanced package.

budget.mixed: Efficiency: {efficiency} pts per 100 ms invested. Positive standout: {best} exceeded expectations. Budget disappointment: Investment in {worst} didn't yield expected returns.
budget.good: Efficiency: {efficiency} pts per 100 ms invested. Positive standout: {best} exceeded expectations. No investment was totally wasted.
budget.bad: Efficiency: {efficiency} pts per 100 ms invested. Budget disappointment: Investment in {worst} didn't yield expected returns.
budget.neutral: Efficiency: {efficiency} pts per 100 ms invested. The budget was spent in a balanced way, but with no major bargains.

bottleneck.none: The package worked harmoniously, with no obvious bottleneck limiting potential.
bottleneck.carTrackFit: The biggest bottleneck of the year was the chassis, which limited performance on most tracks.
bottleneck.engineTrackFit: The engine was the Achilles heel, making straight-line battles difficult and costing precious points.
bottleneck.reliability: The lack of reliability was very costly, with frequent breakdowns preventing good results.

decisiveMoment.none: The team maintained flat scoring, without a breakthrough race.
decisiveMoment.peak: The peak performance happened at the {circuitName} GP, where the team extracted maximum points.

Landing.title: Build a legendary team. Win by milliseconds.
Landing.subtitle: Draft drivers, chassis, engines and team bosses. Simulate a seven-race championship against balanced ghost teams.
Landing.noRandomness: No randomness. Every result is explained.
Landing.engineDesc: Our simulation engine uses deterministic math models. No dice rolls. No AI hallucinations. Every race is calculated based on synergy, track fit, and historical dominance.

Draft.title: Team Configuration
Draft.subtitle: Step {step} of {total}: {stepName}
Draft.stepCarDesc: The chassis dictates aerodynamics, grip, braking, tire wear, and track adaptation.
Draft.stepEngineDesc: The engine impacts straight-line speed, reliability, cooling, and race pace.
Draft.stepPrimaryDesc: Your primary driver leads the team and usually contends for the Drivers' Championship.
Draft.stepSecondaryDesc: The secondary driver is essential for scoring points in the Constructors' Championship.
Draft.stepTPDesc: The team principal affects operational discipline, crisis management, and conflict resolution.
Draft.stepTDDesc: The technical director improves car development, aerodynamics, setup, and reliability.
Draft.stepPhilosophyDesc: The philosophy sets the strategic tone of the season: aggressive, balanced, conservative, or developmental.
Draft.stepReviewDesc: Double-check your choices before simulating the championship.
Draft.formatQuickDesc: Short season with 7 races and 5 ghost teams.
Draft.formatStandardDesc: Full season with 12 races and 10 ghost teams.

Simulate.title: Championship Simulation
Simulate.subtitle: {races} races · {teams} team grid · Deterministic
Simulate.legacyWarning: Warning: This simulation was created in an older version, some explanations might not be available.

results.youFinishedPosition: You finished {position}th in the constructors' standings
results.winsDetail: Race wins scored by your team
results.podiumsDetail: Top 3 finishes scored by your team
results.positionPodium: You finished on the constructors' podium
results.positionTopFive: You finished inside the constructors' top 5
results.positionOutside: You finished {position}th in the constructors' standings

philosophy.headline: Choose team philosophy
philosophy.intro: The philosophy defines how your team will attack the season. It affects performance, risk, reliability, tires and development.

fit.balancedNeutral: A neutral approach with no major clash with the current package.
fit.balancedStability: Good for a team that does not need extra extremes.
fit.aggressivePower: Pushes for poles and wins from a package that can hold it together.
fit.aggressiveRisk: Pace rises, but so does wear, mistakes and retirements.
fit.recommendedReliability: A reliable car and engine make aggression safer.
fit.conservativeStability: Good for limiting losses and protecting points in long races.
fit.conservativeReliability: Helps when the package still lacks full confidence.
fit.qualifyingFocus: Excellent for turning one-lap pace into Saturday advantage.
fit.qualifyingStreet: Best on tracks where overtaking is limited and grid position matters most.
fit.developmentQuickWarning: A short season leaves too little time to recover the early penalty.
fit.developmentGrowth: Starts slower, but improves the package as the season unfolds.
fit.developmentLateSeason: The development gain becomes more visible late in the championship.

balanced.name: Balanced
balanced.description: A balanced approach with no major strengths or weaknesses.
balanced.bestFor: Teams that already have a well-rounded package and do not want extremes.
balanced.risk: Low risk, but no strong bonus in any single area.

aggressive.name: Aggressive
aggressive.description: Chases poles, wins and overtakes while pushing the package to the limit.
aggressive.risk: More wear, more mistakes and more retirements.

conservative.name: Conservative
conservative.description: Prioritizes finishing races, saving tires and scoring steady points.
conservative.risk: Lower chance of a clean-race victory.

qualifying_focused.name: Qualifying focused
qualifying_focused.description: Prioritizes grid position and one-lap performance.
qualifying_focused.bestFor: Tracks where qualifying is decisive and overtaking is hard.
qualifying_focused.risk: Can lose ground in race pace and tire life.

development_focused.name: Development focused
development_focused.description: Sacrifices early pace to evolve the car during the season.
development_focused.bestFor: Long championships with a strong technical director and patient team.
development_focused.risk: Costs more at the start and pays off later.
development_focused.quickWarning: In a short championship there is too little time to recover the early deficit.
